ddls:
CREATE TABLE "emails" (
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the email */,
  "from_address" TEXT /* Email address of the sender */,
  "to_address" TEXT /* Email address of the recipient */,
  "cc_address" TEXT /* Carbon copy email addresses */,
  "bcc_address" TEXT /* Blind carbon copy email addresses */,
  "folder" INTEGER /* Folder where the email is stored */,
  "origin" INTEGER /* Origin of the email */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* File name of the email */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the email */,
  "user" INTEGER /* User who sent or received the email */,
  "labeled" INTEGER /* Indicates if the email has been labeled */,
  "dollar_value" REAL /* Dollar value mentioned in the email */,
  "message_length" INTEGER /* Length of the email message */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time when the email was sent or received */,
  "is_message_long" INTEGER /* Indicates if the email message is long */,
  "row_number" INTEGER /* Row number in the original data source */,
  "importance" INTEGER /* Importance level of the email */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the email has been read */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the email */,
  "is_draft" INTEGER /* Indicates if the email is a draft */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the email has been deleted */,
  "encryption_status" TEXT /* Indicates if the email is encrypted */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the email was sent/received */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to send/receive the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_folder FOREIGN KEY ("folder") REFERENCES folders ("folder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_user FOREIGN KEY ("user") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_labeled FOREIGN KEY ("labeled") REFERENCES email_labels ("label_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_origin FOREIGN KEY ("origin") REFERENCES origins ("origin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "folders" (
  "folder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each folder */,
  "folder_name" TEXT /* Name of the folder */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the folder */,
  "is_shared" INTEGER /* Indicates if the folder is shared */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* List of users or groups with whom the folder is shared */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("folder_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "origins" (
  "origin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each origin */,
  "origin_name" TEXT /* Name of the origin */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the origin */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain of the origin */,
  "is_external" INTEGER /* Indicates if the origin is external to the company */,
  "security_level" TEXT /* Security level of the origin */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("origin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "label_name" TEXT /* Name of the label */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the label */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the label is a default label */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color associated with the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email the attachment belongs to */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* File name of the attachment */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the attachment in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the attachment was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the attachment */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attachment is encrypted */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attachment contains sensitive information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_attachments_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_forwarding" (
  "forwarding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forwarding event */,
  "original_email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original email */,
  "forwarded_email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forwarded email */,
  "forward_date" TEXT /* Date when the email was forwarded */,
  "forwarded_by" INTEGER /* User who forwarded the email */,
  "forwarded_to" INTEGER /* User to whom the email was forwarded */,
  "forwarded_reason" TEXT /* Reason for forwarding the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forwarding_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_forwarding_forwarded_to FOREIGN KEY ("forwarded_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_forwarding_forwarded_by FOREIGN KEY ("forwarded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_forwarding_original_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_forwarding_forwarded_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("forwarded_email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reply event */,
  "original_email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original email */,
  "replied_email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the replied email */,
  "reply_date" TEXT /* Date when the email was replied */,
  "replied_by" INTEGER /* User who replied to the email */,
  "replied_to" INTEGER /* User to whom the email was replied */,
  "reply_reason" TEXT /* Reason for replying to the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_replies_replied_to FOREIGN KEY ("replied_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_replies_replied_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("replied_email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_replies_replied_by FOREIGN KEY ("replied_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_replies_original_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_sentiment" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the email */,
  "sentiment_type" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_details" TEXT /* Detailed sentiment analysis results */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_sentiment_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the email */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the email was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the email */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the email was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_access_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_sharing" (
  "sharing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sharing event */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email being shared */,
  "shared_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user with whom the email is shared */,
  "sharing_date" TEXT /* Date when the email was shared */,
  "shared_by" INTEGER /* User who shared the email */,
  "shared_with" INTEGER /* User with whom the email was shared */,
  "sharing_reason" TEXT /* Reason for sharing the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sharing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_sharing_shared_by FOREIGN KEY ("shared_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_sharing_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_sharing_shared_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("shared_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_sharing_shared_with FOREIGN KEY ("shared_with") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword extracted from the email content */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the keyword in the email */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the keyword is sensitive */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Relevance score of the keyword in the context of the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_keywords_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* User who generated the report */,
  "is_shared" INTEGER /* Indicates if the report is shared */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* List of users or groups with whom the report is shared */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_permissions FOREIGN KEY ("permissions") REFERENCES user_permissions ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_tags_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)
